excerpt: His vision was always farsighted...by his justice and mercy
source_location_hint: Chapter on Zarrar's testimony

excerpt: Ali's status with me is like my head with my body
source_location_hint: hadith collection on Ali's station

excerpt: Looking at Ali's face is worship
source_location_hint: discourse on Ali's virtues

excerpt: dispute among the people as to who was closest to him among the Companions
source_location_hint: account of the Prophet's seating arrangement

excerpt: Ali ibn Abi Talib is the first of the believers
source_location_hint: chapter on precedence in faith

excerpt: I am the first of the believers...Ask me about the knowledge of Allah and the wisdom stored within me
source_location_hint: sermon at the mosque of Kufa after Nahrawan

excerpt: The pure abundance is your successor, Ali—so turn toward him. For your enemy is Abu Bakr
source_location_hint: numerological interpretation of Sura 108

excerpt: Ali's path is the straight path
source_location_hint: numerical and literal interpretation of ʿalī

excerpt: Ali is the inner Qur'an whom the Prophet's people neglected
source_location_hint: interpretation of Surah 25:30

excerpt: I and Ali are of one light
source_location_hint: discussion of the Prophet and Imam's unified essence

excerpt: There was no scholar more learned in the Book than Ali. Indeed, Ali was the first to bear witness to the Messenger of Allah
source_location_hint: commentary on verse of witness

excerpt: Whoever takes me as his master, let him take Ali as his master
source_location_hint: Ghadir Khumm tradition

excerpt: Allah placed our light in Adam's handkerchief. Then we moved through the loins of the righteous and the wombs of the pure until it came to Maulana Abd al-Muttalib
source_location_hint: doctrine of the preserved light

excerpt: Both Muhammad and Ali were born in Mecca; the Prophet was commissioned on the twenty-seventh of Rajab, and Ali was born on the twenty-seventh of Rajab
source_location_hint: shared dates and cities of birth

excerpt: Allah Almighty has created all of His creation in pairs...the tangible things that are visible on the outside, and the reasonable things that are not visible on the inside
source_location_hint: section 8, cosmological metaphysics

excerpt: the Prophet (peace be upon him) also based his religion on the same divine nature and adapted the method of worship and guidance in the same divine style...when a rational person thinks and thinks, he will know that the religion of Islam and the Shariah of the Prophet are the same principles as the creation of the heavens
source_location_hint: section 8, prophetic alignment with creation

excerpt: When Allaah has the power to create something when He intends it, then the question arises: why did it take Him six days to create the universe? This shows that this verse has some other inner meanings
source_location_hint: section 8, six days of creation with taʾwīl

excerpt: I am the city of knowledge and Ali is its gate
source_work: Hadith tradition (widely attested in Ismaili sources)
source_location_hint: Ghadīr Khumm and subsequent transmission

excerpt: Verily, your Walî (Protector or Helper) is Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, - those who perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and give Zakat, and they bow down (submit themselves with obedience to Allah in prayer)
source_location_hint: Qur'ān 5:55

excerpt: a person who follows the six supplications and does not commit himself to the will and wilayat of Amir al-Mu'minin, will not attain the status of perfection
source_location_hint: section 8, hierarchy of perfection

excerpt: Wilayat Ali and goes to the Hereafter despite praying six supplications, he will be counted as dead...As soon as the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allaah be upon him) had fulfilled his last obligation, Allaah said [This day I have perfected for you your religion...]
source_work: Kashkole (as mediated through current text); Quran 5:3
source_authority: Kashkole scholarly compendium and Qur'ānic text
source_location_hint: section 8, wilāyat and religious completion

excerpt: Whomever I am the master and guardian of, ʿAlī is his master and guardian
source_work: Ḥadīth al-Ghadīr (widely attested in Ismaili and other Islamic traditions)
source_location_hint: Ghadīr Khumm declaration

excerpt: When ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿAbbās was asked, 'Who are the relatives of the Prophet whom Allah has mentioned in this verse?' he replied: 'These people are ʿAlī, Fāṭimah, al-Ḥasan, and al-Ḥusayn'
source_location_hint: section 9, identification of Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: ʿAlī is the best of all human beings; whoever denies this is an infidel
source_location_hint: section 9, excellence of ʿAlī

excerpt: Al-Ḥasan and al-Ḥusayn are the true Imāms, whether they rise up to claim their rights or sit quietly in patience, and their father is better than both of them
source_work: Ḥadīth tradition (as cited in Kashkole anecdote)
source_location_hint: section 9, Imam al-Hakim anecdote

excerpt: After this verse was revealed [Quran 5:55], he added his testimony to his caliphate in principle, which was his right...I bear witness that Ali is the guardian of Allah
source_location_hint: section 9, conclusion on wilāyat testimony

excerpt: Yasin certainly refers to the descendants of the Prophet who are the Imāms. Thus, those whom Allah has associated with the Messenger are the children of the Messenger. The best and most proximate to the Prophet is undoubtedly ʿAlī
source_work: Kashkole / As'āf al-Ṭālib (as mediated through current text)
source_location_hint: section 10, second argument, Yasin interpretation

excerpt: All these verses oblige the Prophet (peace be upon him) to follow in the footsteps of his predecessors and make 'Ali (a.s.), his brother, his minister, his minister, and to make him a part of his Prophethood. And the Messenger did exactly that, and Allah gave them the same kingdom which Moses and Aaron had given them
source_location_hint: section 10, fourth argument, Sunnah of Allah

excerpt: It is established that the Ahl al-Bayt (a) of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allaah be upon him) are the best of those who believe in Allah and do good deeds, and their being the best is a clear proof that they are the best of human beings
source_location_hint: section 10, fifth argument, Qur'ān 98:7

excerpt: it is absolutely correct and justified to say that Muhammad and Ali Khair al-Basr and Atrathama Khair al-Attar in our Adhan are absolutely correct, correct and justified. Because Ali is the best and noblest of the Prophet's family!
source_work: Kitāb As'āf al-Ṭālib fī Jāmiʿ al-Muṭṭālib
source_location_hint: section 10, seventh argument, conclusion

excerpt: The eyewitness perceives the matter with his own faculties; the hearing witness knows through the testimony of a just and trustworthy intermediary.
source_location_hint: on types of witnesses and testimony

excerpt: O Messenger, We have sent you as a witness unto Us.
source_location_hint: on the Prophet's role as witness to creation

excerpt: Allah created my light first, and created the entire universe by means of my light.
source_location_hint: on the primordial light of the Prophet

excerpt: I knew not the truth save what I accomplished, and Ali accomplished it with me. None has known me save Allah and Ali.
source_location_hint: on the Prophet's unique relationship with Ali

excerpt: Our matter is your matter, and your matter is Our matter.
source_location_hint: on the correspondence between divine and Alid matters

excerpt: O Ali, you hold toward me the station that Hārūn held toward Mūsā.
source_location_hint: on Ali's rank equivalent to Aaron's station with Moses

excerpt: We have inscribed all things in the Clear Imam. Just as the Qur'an contains all dry and wet things.
source_location_hint: on the correspondence between Qur'an and Imam

excerpt: Ali and I are created from a single light, and you, O Ali, are from that same light.
source_location_hint: on the unity of light between Prophet and Ali

excerpt: Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels and those possessed of knowledge.
source_location_hint: on the witnesses to divine unity

excerpt: Ali is with the Qur'an, and the Qur'an is with Ali; they shall not be parted until they meet me at the Pool of Kawthar.
source_location_hint: on the inseparability of Ali and the Qur'an

excerpt: Knowledge without action is calamity; action without knowledge is misguidance.
source_location_hint: on the necessary unity of knowledge and practice

excerpt: O Ali, you are the leader of this Ummah and its Dhu al-Qarnayn.
source_location_hint: on Ali's role as spiritual ruler of two ages

excerpt: Ali was struck twice: once by Amr ibn Abd Waddad at the Battle of the Trench, and once by Ibn Muljam.
source_location_hint: on the two blows that left horn-like marks on Ali's head

excerpt: Whoever gathers the stones shall regret, and whoever leaves them shall also regret—for in the light they become precious gems.
source_location_hint: allegorical teaching on the value of spiritual knowledge

excerpt: Gog and Magog scratch and lick the wall each night, nearly breaching it; but at dawn the muezzin calls, and the wall returns whole.
source_location_hint: on the cosmic struggle between truth and falsehood

excerpt: The wall of covenant protects believers from those who exaggerate and those who diminish the truth about the Imams.
source_location_hint: on the spiritual protection afforded by the covenant of wilaya

excerpt: In seventy ages no person has attained a station higher than Ali. He is called bald because of his transcendence, great-bellied because his knowledge is boundless.
source_location_hint: on the meanings of Al-Anza al-Bateen

excerpt: I am the slave of the one aided by Jibril and supported by Mikail. All the denizens of heaven praise him.
source_location_hint: Qambar's declaration of Ali's stations and virtues

excerpt: Your river of generosity was in full flood. I feared you might give charity to me as well!
source_location_hint: on Ali's boundless generosity

excerpt: That was Khidr, come to remind you that I am the true Caliph of the Messenger of Allah.
source_location_hint: on the four designated caliphs in Islamic history

excerpt: In Paradise I am Sayyid. Near the Throne I am called Ali. In the heavens I am al-Taqi. On earth I am Amir al-Muminin.
source_location_hint: the multiple designations of Ali across cosmic realms

excerpt: Salman's heart is pure and his tongue cannot speak falsehood. There are a hundred secrets hidden in every word he utters.
source_location_hint: on the depths of Salman's knowledge

excerpt: No one knows Allah except me and Ali. No one knows me except Allah and Ali. I am neither the Messenger nor Allah, so I said I know him not.
source_location_hint: on the mutual knowledge between Salman, the Prophet, and Ali

excerpt: I prostrated to the Light that shines between Ali's eyes, not to his form.
source_location_hint: on the divine light manifest in the Imam

excerpt: This creature they claim as their own is not a camel—it is a she-camel!
source_location_hint: the discovery of the Syrians' deception through linguistic precision

excerpt: Tell your master, Ali ibn Abi Talib, that these Syrians cannot distinguish between a camel and a she-camel. What then can they understand of the difference between Muawiya and Ali?
source_location_hint: Muawiya's acknowledgment of Ali's superiority

excerpt: This watermelon harbored hatred toward me in the realm of intentions, and became bitter. The other loved me and became sweet.
source_location_hint: on the Imam's effect upon creation through his spiritual station
